entries.title: White House officials unveil details of 14-point plan with Iran
entries.description: Senior White House officials detailed the 14-point memorandum of understanding between the U.S. and Iran following President Donald Trump’s remarks at the G7 summit. NBC News reports on whether the U.S. will allow any nuclear program in Iran of any kind and when military personnel could return home from the region.

entries.title: Titan sub: design flaws and company groupthink central to catastrophe, report finds
entries.description: Canadian officials find structural defects in material used for hull and say firm failed to fully test ‘novel’ designCanadian safety officials have issued a damning report on the catastrophic final voyage of the Titan submersible, finding that the US company behind the expedition was overcome by “groupthink” and “confirmation bias” and failed to understand the profound risks confronting their largely untested craft.The 6.7-metre (22ft) carbon fibre submersible dipped below the surface of the Atlantic Ocean in June 2023 en route to the wreckage of the Titanic ocean liner. But nearly two hours after it departed with five passengers, communications went dark. The disappearance prompted a frantic international search, with Canada and the US marshalling all available resources. Continue reading...

entries.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
entries.description: The former Sequoia Capital leader is filling an "existing vacancy" on SpaceX's board, days after the company went public in the largest IPO ever.

entries.title: Chi-Hua Chien saw Facebook coming — now he says the real AI winners won’t be selling AI

entries.title: Warsh announces plan to reshape Federal Reserve
entries.description: The Federal Reserve unanimously voted Wednesday to keep interest rates unchanged. It was the first rate decision under the new chair, Kevin Warsh. CBS News MoneyWatch correspondent Kelly O'Grady has the details.

entries.description: You may think school recess is just time for play, but the American Academy of Pediatrics is re-emphasizing that it's important for kids' health. Bradley Blackburn explains why.

entries.description: ICE recently detained the wife of a retired Army staff sergeant and could deport her, despite her husband's nearly two decades of service to the U.S. CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the story.

entries.title: When will gas prices return to pre-Iran war levels?
entries.description: Oil prices are falling as the end of the war with Iran seems in sight. Arjun Murti, partner at the energy firm Veriten and the author of the Substack newsletter "Super-Spiked," joins to break down when you can expect the pain at the pump to ease.

entries.title: Why Trump told DNI pick Jay Clayton to skip Senate hearing

entries.title: Fed keeps interest rates steady in first decision under Kevin Warsh

entries.title: How Trump's memo of understanding with Iran compares to the Obama nuclear deal
entries.description: The Obama-era Iran deal was packed with technical details and specific requirements limiting, but not shutting down, Iran's nuclear program. President Trump's new agreement is more of a framework, and it's not final yet.

entries.title: Breaking down the powers of a grand jury as DOJ allegedly investigates Gavin Newsom

The DOJ has not commented on Newsom's claim. The alleged investigation has raised questions about the powers of a federal grand jury. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks it all down.

entries.title: Legislation overhauling college sports faces a major test in the Senate
entries.description: A bill that top lawmakers and athletic leaders have described as the best chance to stabilize college sports faces a key test in the Senate on Thursday as sponsors roll out a revised version after weeks of input from schools, conferences and athletes.  The bipartisan Protect College Sports Act aims to regulate payments to players, limit them to one free transfer over their careers and create a rule to restrict coaches from changing jobs during a season.  The Senate Commerce Committee will debate the latest version of the bill on Thursday before potentially voting on whether to send it to the full Senate for consideration.
